import torch
import torch.nn as nn
from torchvision import models

def create_model(num_classes=345, backbone='resnet50'):
    if backbone == 'resnet50':
        model = models.resnet50(weights='IMAGENET1K_V1')
        in_features = model.fc.in_features
        model.fc = nn.Linear(in_features, num_classes)
    elif backbone == 'vit_base_patch16_224.augreg_in1k':
        import timm
        model = timm.create_model('vit_base_patch16_224.augreg_in1k', pretrained=True)
        in_features = model.head.in_features
        model.head = nn.Linear(in_features, num_classes)
    elif backbone == 'vit_base_patch16_clip_224.openai':
        import timm
        model = timm.create_model('vit_base_patch16_clip_224.openai', pretrained=True)
        in_features = model.head.in_features
        model.head = nn.Linear(in_features, num_classes)
    elif backbone == 'dinov2_base':
        from transformers import AutoModel
        model = AutoModel.from_pretrained('facebook/dinov2-base')
        # DINOv2 outputs patch features, we need a classification head
        # DINOv2Base has config.hidden_size = 768, usually pooling is CLS token
        in_features = model.config.hidden_size
        model.classifier = nn.Sequential(
            nn.LayerNorm(in_features),
            nn.Linear(in_features, num_classes)
        )
    else:
        raise ValueError(f'Unsupported backbone: {backbone}')
    return model
